1→package cliui
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"strings"
     7→
     8→	"github.com/coder/coder/v2/codersdk"
     9→	"github.com/coder/pretty"
    10→	"github.com/coder/serpent"
    11→)
    12→
    13→func RichParameter(inv *serpent.Invocation, templateVersionParameter codersdk.TemplateVersionParameter, defaultOverrides map[string]string) (string, error) {
    14→	label := templateVersionParameter.Name
    15→	if templateVersionParameter.DisplayName != "" {
    16→		label = templateVersionParameter.DisplayName
    17→	}
    18→
    19→	if templateVersionParameter.Ephemeral {
    20→		label += pretty.Sprint(DefaultStyles.Warn, " (build option)")
    21→	}
    22→
    23→	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(inv.Stdout, Bold(label))
    24→
    25→	if templateVersionParameter.DescriptionPlaintext != "" {
    26→		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(inv.Stdout, "  "+strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(strings.Split(templateVersionParameter.DescriptionPlaintext, "\n"), "\n  "))+"\n")
    27→	}
    28→
    29→	defaultValue := templateVersionParameter.DefaultValue
    30→	if v, ok := defaultOverrides[templateVersionParameter.Name]; ok {
    31→		defaultValue = v
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	var err error
    35→	var value string
    36→	switch {
    37→	case templateVersionParameter.Type == "list(string)":
    38→		// Move the cursor up a single line for nicer display!
    39→		_, _ = fmt.Fprint(inv.Stdout, "\033[1A")
    40→
    41→		var options []string
    42→		err = json.Unmarshal([]byte(templateVersionParameter.DefaultValue), &options)
    43→		if err != nil {
    44→			return "", err
    45→		}
    46→
    47→		values, err := MultiSelect(inv, MultiSelectOptions{
    48→			Options:  options,
    49→			Defaults: options,
    50→		})
    51→		if err == nil {
    52→			v, err := json.Marshal(&values)
    53→			if err != nil {
    54→				return "", err
    55→			}
    56→
    57→			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(inv.Stdout)
    58→			pretty.Fprintf(
    59→				inv.Stdout,
    60→				DefaultStyles.Prompt, "%s\n", strings.Join(values, ", "),
    61→			)
    62→			value = string(v)
    63→		}
    64→	case len(templateVersionParameter.Options) > 0:
    65→		// Move the cursor up a single line for nicer display!
    66→		_, _ = fmt.Fprint(inv.Stdout, "\033[1A")
    67→		var richParameterOption *codersdk.TemplateVersionParameterOption
    68→		richParameterOption, err = RichSelect(inv, RichSelectOptions{
    69→			Options:    templateVersionParameter.Options,
    70→			Default:    defaultValue,
    71→			HideSearch: true,
    72→		})
    73→		if err == nil {
    74→			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(inv.Stdout)
    75→			pretty.Fprintf(inv.Stdout, DefaultStyles.Prompt, "%s\n", richParameterOption.Name)
    76→			value = richParameterOption.Value
    77→		}
    78→	default:
    79→		text := "Enter a value"
    80→		if !templateVersionParameter.Required {
    81→			text += fmt.Sprintf(" (default: %q)", defaultValue)
    82→		}
    83→		text += ":"
    84→
    85→		value, err = Prompt(inv, PromptOptions{
    86→			Text: Bold(text),
    87→			Validate: func(value string) error {
    88→				return validateRichPrompt(value, templateVersionParameter)
    89→			},
    90→		})
    91→		value = strings.TrimSpace(value)
    92→	}
    93→	if err != nil {
    94→		return "", err
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	// If they didn't specify anything, use the default value if set.
    98→	if len(templateVersionParameter.Options) == 0 && value == "" {
    99→		value = defaultValue
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	return value, nil
   103→}
   104→
   105→func validateRichPrompt(value string, p codersdk.TemplateVersionParameter) error {
   106→	return codersdk.ValidateWorkspaceBuildParameter(p, &codersdk.WorkspaceBuildParameter{
   107→		Name:  p.Name,
   108→		Value: value,
   109→	}, nil)
   110→}
   111→

1→package ptytest
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"bufio"
     5→	"bytes"
     6→	"context"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"io"
     9→	"regexp"
    10→	"runtime"
    11→	"slices"
    12→	"strings"
    13→	"sync"
    14→	"testing"
    15→	"time"
    16→	"unicode/utf8"
    17→
    18→	"github.com/acarl005/stripansi"
    19→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    20→	"golang.org/x/xerrors"
    21→
    22→	"github.com/coder/coder/v2/pty"
    23→	"github.com/coder/coder/v2/testutil"
    24→	"github.com/coder/serpent"
    25→)
    26→
    27→func New(t *testing.T, opts ...pty.Option) *PTY {
    28→	t.Helper()
    29→
    30→	ptty, err := pty.New(opts...)
    31→	require.NoError(t, err)
    32→
    33→	e := newExpecter(t, ptty.Output(), "cmd")
    34→	r := &PTY{
    35→		outExpecter: e,
    36→		PTY:         ptty,
    37→	}
    38→	// Ensure pty is cleaned up at the end of test.
    39→	t.Cleanup(func() {
    40→		_ = r.Close()
    41→	})
    42→	return r
    43→}
    44→
    45→// Start starts a new process asynchronously and returns a PTYCmd and Process.
    46→// It kills the process and PTYCmd upon cleanup
    47→func Start(t *testing.T, cmd *pty.Cmd, opts ...pty.StartOption) (*PTYCmd, pty.Process) {
    48→	t.Helper()
    49→
    50→	ptty, ps, err := pty.Start(cmd, opts...)
    51→	require.NoError(t, err)
    52→	t.Cleanup(func() {
    53→		_ = ps.Kill()
    54→		_ = ps.Wait()
    55→	})
    56→	ex := newExpecter(t, ptty.OutputReader(), cmd.Args[0])
    57→
    58→	r := &PTYCmd{
    59→		outExpecter: ex,
    60→		PTYCmd:      ptty,
    61→	}
    62→	t.Cleanup(func() {
    63→		_ = r.Close()
    64→	})
    65→	return r, ps
    66→}
    67→
    68→func newExpecter(t *testing.T, r io.Reader, name string) outExpecter {
    69→	// Use pipe for logging.
    70→	logDone := make(chan struct{})
    71→	logr, logw := io.Pipe()
    72→
    73→	// Write to log and output buffer.
    74→	copyDone := make(chan struct{})
    75→	out := newStdbuf()
    76→	w := io.MultiWriter(logw, out)
    77→
    78→	ex := outExpecter{
    79→		t:    t,
    80→		out:  out,
    81→		name: name,
    82→
    83→		runeReader: bufio.NewReaderSize(out, utf8.UTFMax),
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	logClose := func(name string, c io.Closer) {
    87→		ex.logf("closing %s", name)
    88→		err := c.Close()
    89→		ex.logf("closed %s: %v", name, err)
    90→	}
    91→	// Set the actual close function for the outExpecter.
    92→	ex.close = func(reason string) error {
    93→		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), testutil.WaitShort)
    94→		defer cancel()
    95→
    96→		ex.logf("closing expecter: %s", reason)
    97→
    98→		// Caller needs to have closed the PTY so that copying can complete
    99→		select {
   100→		case <-ctx.Done():
   101→			ex.fatalf("close", "copy did not close in time")
   102→		case <-copyDone:
   103→		}
   104→
   105→		logClose("logw", logw)
   106→		logClose("logr", logr)
   107→		select {
   108→		case <-ctx.Done():
   109→			ex.fatalf("close", "log pipe did not close in time")
   110→		case <-logDone:
   111→		}
   112→
   113→		ex.logf("closed expecter")
   114→
   115→		return nil
   116→	}
   117→
   118→	go func() {
   119→		defer close(copyDone)
   120→		_, err := io.Copy(w, r)
   121→		ex.logf("copy done: %v", err)
   122→		ex.logf("closing out")
   123→		err = out.closeErr(err)
   124→		ex.logf("closed out: %v", err)
   125→	}()
   126→
   127→	// Log all output as part of test for easier debugging on errors.
   128→	go func() {
   129→		defer close(logDone)
   130→		s := bufio.NewScanner(logr)
   131→		for s.Scan() {
   132→			ex.logf("%q", stripansi.Strip(s.Text()))
   133→		}
   134→	}()
   135→
   136→	return ex
   137→}
   138→
   139→type outExpecter struct {
   140→	t     *testing.T

content.content: ok  	github.com/coder/coder/v2/cli/cliui	0.074s

content.text: Fixed both MultiSelect-related bugs. - **Bug 1 (options missing when default not set / empty array):** - Root cause was `RichParameter()` building the MultiSelect `Options` list by unmarshalling `templateVersionParameter.DefaultValue` (wrong field) and also using that as `Defaults`. - Fixed `cli/cliui/parameter.go` to: - Unmarshal **available options** from `templateVersionParameter.DefaultValue` - Unmarshal **defaults** from the computed `defaultValue` override (and treat empty string as “no defaults”) - **Bug 2 (MultiSelect ignores descriptions):** - Extended `cliui.MultiSelectOptions` with `OptionsDesc map[string]string` - Updated MultiSelect rendering to display `option: description` when provided. Validation: - Added temporary tests to reproduce both issues, ran `go test ./cli/cliui -run TestMultiSelect`, then **deleted the temporary tests** as required. - Re-ran the remaining MultiSelect tests to confirm everything still passes.

trajectory.result: Fixed both MultiSelect-related bugs. - **Bug 1 (options missing when default not set / empty array):** - Root cause was `RichParameter()` building the MultiSelect `Options` list by unmarshalling `templateVersionParameter.DefaultValue` (wrong field) and also using that as `Defaults`. - Fixed `cli/cliui/parameter.go` to: - Unmarshal **available options** from `templateVersionParameter.DefaultValue` - Unmarshal **defaults** from the computed `defaultValue` override (and treat empty string as “no defaults”) - **Bug 2 (MultiSelect ignores descriptions):** - Extended `cliui.MultiSelectOptions` with `OptionsDesc map[string]string` - Updated MultiSelect rendering to display `option: description` when provided. Validation: - Added temporary tests to reproduce both issues, ran `go test ./cli/cliui -run TestMultiSelect`, then **deleted the temporary tests** as required. - Re-ran the remaining MultiSelect tests to confirm everything still passes.
